accounts.name: swig
docs: Position 0 — REQUIRED at this index by Swig's ProgramExec authority
validator. When a Swig::SignV2 follows this instruction in the same
transaction, Swig's on-chain validator inspects accounts[0..1] of the
preceding instruction and rejects unless they're [swig, swig_wallet].
We additionally enforce `swig.key() == vault.swig_address` via the
`address` constraint so a caller cannot pass an arbitrary Swig account
in here — defense in depth: even if Swig's own validation changes in a
future program upgrade, this vault keeps its own invariant.
deserialize or dereference it.

accounts.name: swig_wallet_address
docs: Position 1 — required by Swig's ProgramExec validator (see `swig`).
The Swig wallet address is the PDA owning the SPL token ATA being
debited; it is derived under the Swig program at
`["swig-wallet-address", swig_pubkey]`.
We independently verify the canonical derivation via Anchor's `seeds`
+ `seeds::program` constraint. If a caller supplied a fake account, our
program rejects before any Swig CPI runs — we do not rely on Swig
catching it downstream.

accounts.name: instructions_sysvar
docs: buyer's passkey signature via the SIMD-0075 precompile sibling.

accounts.name: dexter_authority
docs: The Dexter session authority to bind to this vault. Must sign init, so
a vault can only be created bound to an authority that consented. This
key may later mutate `pending_voucher_count` (settle_voucher /
force_release) — and only this key. It can never move funds.

accounts.name: vault
docs: Read-only: this instruction proves passkey control and mutates NOTHING.

accounts.name: instructions_sysvar
docs: passkey signature via the SIMD-0075 precompile sibling instruction.

accounts.name: vault
docs: Receives the new `active_session`. Mutated, no signer required: the
passkey signature embedded in the args (verified via the SIMD-0075
precompile sibling) is what authorizes the mutation.

accounts.name: instructions_sysvar
docs: instruction in the transaction MUST be a secp256r1_verify call whose
signed message is `authenticatorData || sha256(clientDataJSON)` and
whose `clientDataJSON.challenge` decodes to sha256(registration_message).

accounts.name: vault
docs: Mutated to clear `active_session`. No signer required; the passkey
signature in the args is the authorization.

accounts.name: instructions_sysvar
docs: instruction in the transaction MUST be a secp256r1_verify call.

accounts.name: dexter_authority
docs: Must equal the vault's CURRENT `dexter_authority`. Only the current
authority can hand off to a new one — so the session-master key can be
rotated without bricking existing vaults.

accounts.name: instructions_sysvar
docs: CURRENT passkey signature via the SIMD-0075 precompile sibling.

accounts.name: vault
docs: The dexter-vault PDA — initialized by initialize_vault, mutated here
(we set swig_address).

accounts.name: fee_payer
docs: The fee payer + role-0 bootstrap authority. MUST be a tx-level signer.

accounts.name: swig_account
docs: The Swig state account, derived as `findProgramAddress([swig_id], swig_program)`.
Swig will initialize it during the CreateV1 CPI.

accounts.name: swig_wallet_address
docs: The Swig wallet PDA (different from swig_account — it's the spending
authority address). CPI'd into by CreateV1.

accounts.name: swig_program
docs: The Swig program itself.

accounts.name: system_program
docs: System program — Swig CreateV1 needs it to create the state account.

accounts.name: instructions_sysvar
docs: Instructions sysvar — read by verify_passkey_signed.

accounts.name: swig
docs: Position 0 — REQUIRED at this index by Swig's ProgramExec authority
validator. When a Swig::SignV2 follows this instruction in the same
transaction, Swig's on-chain validator inspects accounts[0..1] of
the preceding instruction and rejects unless they're
[swig, swig_wallet], AND that the preceding instruction's data
starts with a registered marker discriminator.
Also bound to `vault.swig_address` via the Anchor `address`
constraint, so a caller cannot pass an arbitrary Swig account here.
deserialize or dereference it.

accounts.name: swig_wallet_address
docs: Position 1 — required by Swig's ProgramExec validator (see `swig`).
The Swig wallet address is the PDA owning the SPL token ATA being
debited; derived under the Swig program at
`["swig-wallet-address", swig_pubkey]`.

accounts.name: dexter_authority
docs: Must equal `vault.dexter_authority` — only the recorded authority
can drive the gate-counter decrement. The buyer's session-key
signature is what authorizes the SPEND amount; this signer is what
authorizes the counter mutation. Same model as the existing
`settle_voucher`. NOTE: this signer does NOT sign the Swig transfer
in [N+1] — that's signed by the swig wallet PDA via Swig's
ProgramExec authority, gated by the vault program being the
ProgramExec authority on the Swig.

accounts.name: dexter_authority
docs: Must equal the `dexter_authority` recorded on the vault at init.
`has_one` enforces this — closing Finding B (previously any signer
could mutate the counter).

errors.name: PendingVouchersExist
errors.msg: Pending vouchers must settle before withdrawal can finalize

errors.name: InvalidVoucherSignature
errors.msg: Voucher signature does not match Dexter session key

errors.name: ForceReleaseTooEarly
errors.msg: force_release grace period has not elapsed

errors.name: UnsupportedVaultVersion
errors.msg: Vault account version is not supported by this program

errors.name: SessionAlreadyActive
errors.msg: A session is already active on this vault and has not expired

errors.name: SessionExpiryInPast
errors.msg: Session expiry must be in the future

errors.name: SessionCapZero
errors.msg: Session max_amount must be greater than zero

errors.name: SessionPubkeyMismatch
errors.msg: Revocation message session pubkey does not match the active session

fields.name: client_data_json
docs: WebAuthn clientDataJSON; challenge must be sha256(operation_message).

fields.name: client_data_json
docs: WebAuthn clientDataJSON; challenge must be sha256(operation_message).

fields.name: cooling_off_seconds
docs: Withdrawal cooling-off in seconds. Zero = instant. See §7.1 of the v2
design doc for why this tightened from i64 to u32.

fields.name: identity_claim
docs: Operator-defined opaque identity bytes. The protocol doesn't interpret
these. Dexter writes a Supabase UUID into the first 16 bytes and zeros
the rest; future operators may use whichever scheme they want.

fields.name: challenge
docs: The 32-byte challenge to prove control over (e.g. a SIWX login nonce /
digest). The passkey must have signed `"siwx_login" || challenge`.

fields.name: client_data_json
docs: WebAuthn clientDataJSON; its `challenge` field must base64url-decode to
`sha256("siwx_login" || challenge)`.

fields.name: session_pubkey
docs: Ed25519 pubkey the buyer's SDK generated in memory. The passkey is
endorsing this exact key — only this key can sign vouchers for the
duration of the session.

fields.name: max_amount
docs: Cumulative cap in atomic units. The seller's middleware AND any future
on-chain consumer of `active_session.spent` enforces this.

fields.name: expires_at
docs: Wall-clock expiry, unix seconds. Must be strictly in the future.

fields.name: allowed_counterparty
docs: The seller this session is bound to. Any voucher claiming a different
counterparty MUST be rejected by the seller's verification path.

fields.name: nonce
docs: Per-session nonce. Combined with `expires_at`, gives each session a
unique fingerprint for off-chain replay protection. Caller picks; the
program does not enforce monotonicity (a non-monotonic nonce is the
buyer's own footgun, not a protocol attack).

fields.name: client_data_json
docs: WebAuthn `clientDataJSON`. Its `challenge` field must base64url-decode
to sha256(registration_message).

fields.name: authenticator_data
docs: WebAuthn `authenticatorData` (37+ bytes).

fields.name: client_data_json
docs: WebAuthn `clientDataJSON` from the browser. Its `challenge` field
must base64url-decode to `sha256(operation_message)`.

fields.name: authenticator_data
docs: WebAuthn `authenticatorData` (37+ bytes).

fields.name: client_data_json
docs: WebAuthn clientDataJSON; challenge must be sha256(operation_message).

types.name: SessionRegistration
docs: On-chain record of an authorized session key.
The session pubkey is an ordinary ed25519 keypair the buyer's SDK generated
in memory at tab-open time. The passkey signed a 180-byte registration
message (see docs/DESIGN-vault-v2-session-keys.md §2.2) endorsing these
scope limits. From this point on, the seller's middleware accepts vouchers
signed by `session_pubkey` for this vault, up to `max_amount` and before
`expires_at`, only for `allowed_counterparty`.
`spent` is the running cumulative — incremented by settle paths that close
vouchers — so we can enforce the cap across the lifetime of the session
without an additional read.

fields.name: client_data_json
docs: WebAuthn `clientDataJSON` produced by the browser. Must contain a
`challenge` field equal to base64url(sha256(operation_message)).

fields.name: authenticator_data
docs: WebAuthn `authenticatorData` produced by the authenticator (37+ bytes).

fields.name: swig_id
docs: 32-byte Swig ID (HMAC-derived client-side from identity_seed + hmac_key).

fields.name: swig_account_bump
docs: Bump for swig_account.

fields.name: swig_wallet_address_bump
docs: Bump for swig_wallet_address PDA.

fields.name: dexter_master_pubkey
docs: Becomes role-2 (Ed25519Session) authority.

fields.name: client_data_json
docs: WebAuthn clientDataJSON (challenge = sha256("set_swig" || swig_address_bytes)).

fields.name: authenticator_data
docs: WebAuthn authenticatorData (37+ bytes).

fields.name: channel_id
docs: Channel id from the voucher's payload — first 32 bytes of the
44-byte message the session key signed.

fields.name: cumulative_amount
docs: Total cumulative amount this voucher authorizes. Must be > the
vault's recorded `active_session.spent` (monotonic) and <= the
session's `max_amount` cap.

fields.name: sequence_number
docs: Monotonic sequence number from the voucher payload. Stored as-is in
the signed message; not currently used for replay defense (the
`spent` monotonicity check covers replay) but reserved for future
out-of-order voucher detection.

fields.name: version
docs: Layout version. MUST be the first field so byte 0 of the deserialized
account directly indicates which Vault generation this is. A program
bound to v2 rejects (`VaultError::UnsupportedVaultVersion`) anything
that isn't `VAULT_VERSION_V2`.

fields.name: cooling_off_seconds
docs: Minimum delay between `request_withdrawal` and `finalize_withdrawal`.
`u32` because negative is meaningless and 136 years of seconds is plenty.

fields.name: identity_claim
docs: Operator-defined opaque identity claim (formerly `supabase_user_id`).
The protocol does not interpret these bytes; Dexter writes a Supabase
UUID prefix, future operators may write whatever they want. Documented
in the OTS spec as "operator-defined".

fields.name: dexter_authority
docs: The session authority recorded at init — the ONLY key permitted to
mutate `pending_voucher_count` (settle_voucher / force_release).

fields.name: active_session
docs: Currently-authorized session key, if any. Written by `register_session_key`
(passkey-signed), cleared by `revoke_session_key` (passkey-signed) or by
the program when expiry is observed during a future read. v2 enforces
at most one active session per vault; multi-seller / multi-session is
future work (issue #5).
